value_picks.name: ServiceNow, Inc.

fcf_yield.detail: FCF $5.108B / market cap $105.317B = 4.85%, in the 4-7% borderline band.

pe_discount.detail: Implied PE = target_median 137.5 / forward_eps 5.02675 = 27.4x vs forward_pe 20.3x = 25.7% discount, in the 15-30% borderline band.

drawdown.detail: Down 51.71% from 52w high of $211.48 — well past the 35% pass threshold.

balance_sheet.detail: Net cash: total_cash $5.182B - total_debt $2.431B = +$2.751B. Passes on the net-cash leg (D/E 20.7 but current_ratio 0.845 is below 1.2, so the second leg would not have passed alone).

dividend.detail: No dividend (payout_ratio 0.0, no dividend_yield field). Treated as neutral = borderline per framework.

institutional.detail: Institutions hold 88.45% and recommendation_mean 1.4375 (<2.5). Buy ratio 43/48 (9 strong buy + 34 buy).

ai_exposure.detail: Enterprise workflow automation is a direct beneficiary of generative AI; the Now Assist GenAI layer is a stated growth and pricing-uplift driver. 22.1% revenue growth at 76.6% gross margins supports the AI-monetization narrative.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in input. Short interest is rising fast — shares_short jumped from 38.95M to 57.94M (+48.78%), now 5.63% of float, though short_ratio is only 2.11 days. Rapid short build into a deeply drawn-down high-multiple name; modest squeeze fuel but no covering pressure given low days-to-cover.

catalysts_general: Trading 51.7% below 52-week high while revenue still growing 22.1% YoY
PEG 0.97 with forward PE 20.3x — growth roughly at par with multiple
Net cash balance sheet (+$2.75B) and $5.1B annual FCF
Analyst conviction strong: recommendation_mean 1.44, 43 of 48 buy/strong buy, median target $137.50 (~35% above price)
Price reclaimed the 20-day SMA (94.58) and is +14.88% over 20 days — possible base formation

downside_scenarios.scenario: Pullback to the 20-day SMA base (~$94.58) overshoots; lifts FCF yield toward ~5.6%.
Retest of the 52-week low on broad software multiple compression.
Bear case: revenue growth decelerates from 22% toward mid-teens and the stock de-rates to a low-teens forward multiple.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Buying near the 20-day SMA base lifts FCF yield from 4.85% toward ~5.6% and widens the discount to the $137.50 median target to ~35%, improving an otherwise only-borderline value setup while still owning a 22%-grower with net cash.

comparables.note: External peer (not in input data). Salesforce is the closest large-cap enterprise SaaS comp; NOW carries a premium multiple justified by faster growth and net-cash balance sheet.
External peer (not in input data). Workday is a HR/finance workflow comp with similar SaaS economics.
External peer (not in input data). Mature high-margin software comp; useful as a floor reference for where high-quality SaaS multiples settle.

value_picks.analyst_take: ServiceNow scores 4.5/6 — passing on drawdown, balance sheet, and institutional conviction, borderline on FCF yield and PE discount. A 22%-growing, net-cash, 76% gross-margin franchise marked down 52% with PEG ~1 and near-unanimous analyst support is an attractive growth-at-a-reasonable-price entry. BUY, but the value cushion is only borderline at $102; entries near $90 materially improve the margin of safety.

value_picks.name: Boston Scientific Corporation

fcf_yield.detail: FCF $2.807B / market cap $75.001B = 3.74%, below the 4% threshold.

pe_discount.detail: Implied PE = target_median 82.5 / forward_eps 3.75582 = 22.0x vs forward_pe 13.4x = 38.8% discount, past the 30% pass threshold.

drawdown.detail: Down 53.92% from 52w high of $109.50, and sitting just above the 52w low of $50.04.

balance_sheet.detail: Net debt (cash $1.453B - debt $11.029B = -$9.576B), so fails the net-cash leg, but passes the second leg: D/E 42.25 (<50) AND current_ratio 1.9 (>1.2).

dividend.detail: No dividend (payout_ratio 0.0, no dividend_yield field). Treated as neutral = borderline per framework.

institutional.detail: Institutions hold 94.92% and recommendation_mean 1.36364 (<2.5). Buy ratio 31/33 (8 strong buy + 23 buy), zero sells.

ai_exposure.detail: Core revenue is physical interventional devices (cardiovascular, MedSurg), insulated from AI disruption. Mild tailwind from AI-assisted electrophysiology mapping and diagnostics, but not a needle-mover for the franchise; AI is neither a threat nor a primary driver.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in input. Short interest is light — 1.93% of float, short_ratio just 1.47 days — and the +21.73% rise in shares_short (23.53M to 28.64M) is off a tiny base. No meaningful short-driven setup; the move is fundamentals/multiple-driven, not positioning.

catalysts_general: Forward PE 13.4x sits 38.8% below the analyst-target-implied 22.0x — the widest PE discount of the three names
Down 53.9% from high, trading within ~1% of its 52-week low ($50.04)
Earnings growth reported at 100% (earnings_growth 1.0) with 11.6% revenue growth and PEG 0.70
Healthiest near-term liquidity of the group: current_ratio 1.9
Near-unanimous analyst support: recommendation_mean 1.36, 31 of 33 buy/strong buy, median target $82.50 (~64% upside)

downside_scenarios.scenario: Marginal break to a fresh 52-week low — the stock is already there, so limited additional air pocket.
De-rate to ~12x forward on med-device multiple compression or a procedure-volume / guidance disappointment.
Bear case: leverage ($11B debt) plus a growth scare pulls the multiple to ~11x forward.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Just below the $50.04 52-week low gives a margin-of-safety entry, lifts FCF yield toward ~3.9% and widens an already-best-in-group ~40%+ PE discount. With 11.6% revenue growth, current_ratio 1.9, and zero analyst sells, the only soft spot is FCF yield, so paying down near the low is the disciplined entry.

comparables.note: External peer (not in input data). Medtronic is the large-cap medical-device benchmark; BSX typically trades at a growth premium to MDT, so BSX at 13.4x forward is notably compressed.
External peer (not in input data). Edwards Lifesciences is a structural-heart comp relevant to BSX's cardiovascular segment.
External peer (not in input data). Stryker is a MedSurg-adjacent comp; useful for where premium-growth device multiples sit.

value_picks.analyst_take: Boston Scientific scores 4.5/6 — the lone fail is FCF yield (3.74%), offset by the widest PE discount of the three (~39%), a 54% drawdown, the best liquidity (CR 1.9), and 94.9% institutional ownership with zero analyst sells. A double-digit-growth, high-margin device franchise trading at 13.4x forward near its 52-week low is a compelling value-plus-quality setup. BUY, ideally accumulated near $48.

value_picks.name: Otis Worldwide Corporation

fcf_yield.detail: FCF $1.682B / market cap $27.547B = 6.11%, in the 4-7% borderline band.

pe_discount.detail: Implied PE = target_median 93.5 / forward_eps 4.71799 = 19.8x vs forward_pe 15.2x = 23.2% discount, in the 15-30% borderline band.

drawdown.detail: Down 29.22% from 52w high of $101.42 — the shallowest drawdown of the three, in the 20-35% borderline band.

balance_sheet.detail: debt_to_equity is missing from the input and book_value is negative (-14.726, so equity is negative and D/E is not meaningful). Net cash leg fails (cash $0.834B - debt $8.218B = -$7.384B net debt), and current_ratio 0.837 is not below 0.8, so the pass/borderline/fail split hinges on D/E — which is absent. Cannot evaluate without estimating; per rules, marked insufficient_data.

dividend.detail: dividend_yield 2.37% (>2%) AND payout_ratio 44.68% (<70%) — sustainable and the only paid dividend of the three.

institutional.detail: Institutions hold 94.625% and recommendation_mean 2.4 (<2.5), so passes literally — but marginally: buy ratio is only 6/15 (2 strong buy + 4 buy) with 8 holds and 1 strong sell.

ai_exposure.detail: Core business is mechanical elevator/escalator manufacturing and a service annuity, largely insulated from AI. Modest tailwind from IoT predictive-maintenance (Otis ONE) improving service-segment retention and margins, but AI is not material to the demand story; net neutral.

deal_radar.assessment: No options_history or capex_signals in input. Short interest 4.59% of float with the highest short_ratio of the group (4.55 days), and shares_short rose +32.67% (11.58M to 15.37M). Rising shorts plus the most days-to-cover suggest growing skepticism (China new-equipment weakness), though days-to-cover gives some squeeze potential on a positive surprise.

catalysts_general: Best FCF yield of the three at 6.11% and a sustainable 2.37% dividend (44.68% payout)
Forward PE 15.2x vs target-implied 19.8x = ~23% discount; median target $93.50 (~30% upside)
Earnings growth reported at 42.6% on 6.4% revenue growth (service-mix margin lift)
Negative book value (-14.726) and net debt of ~$7.38B — leveraged, capital-return-driven equity structure
Weakest analyst posture of the group: recommendation_mean 2.4, 8 holds and 1 strong sell out of 15

downside_scenarios.scenario: Retest of the 52-week low ($69.73), which is ~3% below current price.
China new-equipment weakness deepens and the new-equipment segment drags; de-rate toward ~14x forward.
Bear case: cyclical construction downturn plus the leverage overhang compress the multiple to ~13x forward.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Below the $69.73 52-week low pushes FCF yield above ~6.5% and the dividend yield toward ~2.5%, paying for the leverage/negative-equity risk. Otis is a quality service-annuity compounder, but at $71.79 it is the least dislocated name (only 29% off high) with the softest analyst support, so it warrants patience rather than a chase.

comparables.note: External peer (not in input data). KONE is the closest pure-play elevator comp; relevant for service-mix margin and China-exposure read-through.
External peer (not in input data). Carrier is a fellow UTC spin-off building-systems comp with similar capital structure history.
External peer (not in input data). Trane is a building-equipment comp; useful for where higher-quality industrial-machinery multiples sit.

value_picks.analyst_take: Otis scores 3.5/6 — borderline on FCF yield, PE discount, and drawdown, a clean pass on its sustainable 2.37% dividend, and a marginal institutional pass undercut by 8 holds and a strong sell. The balance-sheet criterion is insufficient_data: D/E is missing and equity is negative (~$7.4B net debt), a genuine risk the framework can't score. A high-quality elevator-service annuity, but the least dislocated and least value-cushioned of the three. WATCHLIST, with interest building below $67.
